pr.title: Match more functions and data in `ftCo_Damage`
pr.author: mchen91

summary: PR #2245 matched additional code and `.sdata2` data in `ftCo_Damage`, touching `ftCo_Damage.c` and `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`. The bot report records 6 new matches, including `ftCo_DamageFly_Anim`, `ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim`, `ftCo_DamageFly_Phys`, `ftCo_8008E5A4`, `ftCo_8008E9D0`, and `.sdata2`, plus improvements to `ftCo_8008EC90` and `ftCo_8008EB58`.

key_files.role: Main source file for ftCommon damage matching changes; refactored several damage/fly/fly-roll functions and helpers.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Damage.c added 73 deleted 82 hunks 11
raw_diff: ftCo_Damage.c hunks around ftCo_8008E5A4, ftCo_8008E9D0, ftCo_Damage_Anim, ftCo_DamageFly_Anim, ftCo_DamageFly_Phys, ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim, ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Phys
text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report lists new matches and improvements in main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Damage

key_files.role: Symbol metadata updates for `.sdata2` float/double labels used by the matched unit.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: config/GALE01/symbols.txt added 7 deleted 7 hunks 2
text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report lists `.sdata2` as a new 100.00% match

what_changed.text: Updated symbol names in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` for several `.sdata2` float/double entries, replacing address-style `lbl_...` names with numbered `@...` labels.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt hunks at .sdata2:0x804D84D0 through .sdata2:0x804D84F8 and .sdata2:0x804DA718

what_changed.text: Refactored `ftCo_8008E5A4` local variable structure for knockback and stick-vector math, including direct use of `fp->input.lstick` fields, `PSVECCrossProduct`, and separated `scale` computation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_8008E5A4 hunk

what_changed.text: Changed `ftCo_8008E9D0` to compute `scaled_kb = ftCo_ScaleBy154(fp->dmg.kb_applied)` and call `ftCo_8008DA4C(gobj, fp->dmg.x1860_element, ftCo_8008D8E8(scaled_kb))`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_8008E9D0 hunk

what_changed.text: Replaced multiple direct `gobj->user_data` local initializations with `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` in helpers and damage animation/physics functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: inlineF0, ftCo_8008F744, inlineC0, ftCo_Damage_Anim, inlineD0, ftCo_DamageFly_Anim, doFlyRoll, ftCo_DamageFly_Phys, ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim, ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Phys hunks

what_changed.text: Added `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` around `ftCo_Damage_SetMv8FromKbThreshold`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_Damage_SetMv8FromKbThreshold hunk

what_changed.text: Changed `inlineD0` to guard the decrement path with `if (fp->mv.co.damage.x8 != 0)`, add a 12-byte padding local, pass `&trajectory` directly to `efAsync_Spawn`, and call `ftCo_Damage_SetMv8FromKbThreshold` inside the zero-threshold branch.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: inlineD0 hunk

what_changed.text: Factored shared air-physics dispatch into new helper `inlineE0`, used by both `ftCo_DamageFly_Phys` and `ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Phys`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: new static void inlineE0 and call sites in ftCo_DamageFly_Phys / ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Phys

what_changed.text: Changed `ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim` to use `PAD_STACK(8)` and always call `inlineD0(gobj)`, relying on `inlineD0`'s internal `x8` guard.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim hunk

smart_moves.text: Shared the duplicated air physics branch through `inlineE0`, reducing repeated `x221C_b6` dispatch code in the two fly physics functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: inlineE0 addition and replacement in ftCo_DamageFly_Phys / ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Phys

smart_moves.text: Moved the `x8` countdown guard into `inlineD0`, letting callers call the helper consistently while preserving the zero-check behavior inside the helper.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: inlineD0 and ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim hunks

smart_moves.text: Used the project accessor macro `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` in many touched functions instead of repeating direct `gobj->user_data` casts, where the diff shows the accessor form was needed for the matched source shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: multiple GET_FIGHTER replacements
text_corpus: bot report confirms several touched functions reached 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: For closely related animation/physics functions, extracting a small static helper for a repeated conditional dispatch can be part of the matching source shape, not just cleanup.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: new inlineE0 used by both physics functions
text_corpus: bot report lists ftCo_DamageFly_Phys as a new 100.00% match

decomp_lessons.lesson: When caller-specific guards interfere with matching, moving the guard into an inline helper can allow multiple callers to use the same helper shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: inlineD0 gains `if (fp->mv.co.damage.x8 != 0)` and ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim changes to always call inlineD0
text_corpus: bot report lists ftCo_DamageFly_Anim and ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim as new 100.00% matches

decomp_lessons.lesson: Data symbol metadata may need to be updated alongside code when the matched unit includes `.sdata2` progress, but such changes should stay tied to explicit data-match evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `.sdata2` symbol renames
text_corpus: bot report lists `.sdata2` +18 bytes, 72.73% to 100.00%

naming_conventions.text: New temporary/local names include `scaled_kb`, `trajectory`, and helper names `inlineD0`/`inlineE0`; unresolved helper names remain address/inline-style rather than speculative semantic names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_8008E9D0 scaled_kb; inlineD0 trajectory; new inlineE0

naming_conventions.text: Symbol metadata changes use numbered `@...` labels for `.sdata2` entries instead of address-style `lbl_...` labels in this PR.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt replacements @216, @220, @320, @321, @460, @521, @637

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped pragma used to prevent inlining of `ftCo_Damage_SetMv8FromKbThreshold`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, function body, `#pragma pop`
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Current standards treat pragmas as exceptional; the PR evidence has a bot match report but no human explanation of why the pragma was unavoidable.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Stack padding used narrowly in `ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim` via `PAD_STACK(8)` after removing two manual padding arrays.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim removes `u8 _0[8]` and `u8 _1[0x18]`, adds `PAD_STACK(8)`
text_corpus: bot report lists ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim as a new 100.00% match
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: PAD_STACK is an explicit matching tactic; the available evidence supports that it accompanied a match but does not explain alternatives tried.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Local declaration order and expression restructuring were adjusted in math-heavy code in `ftCo_8008E5A4`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_8008E5A4 hunk
text_corpus: bot report lists ftCo_8008E5A4 as 90.12% to 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The exact codegen reason is inferred from the diff and match report, not from author commentary.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used a helper extraction (`inlineE0`) to share a repeated branch shape between two physics functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: inlineE0 addition and call sites
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The helper name is generic and no review text explains intended naming.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 shows matched code 55.55% (+0.05%, +1892 bytes), matched data 34.71% (+0.01%, +64 bytes), 6 new matches, and 2 improvements in unmatched items.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: issue_comment by decomp-dev[bot] at 2026-03-09T02:53:23Z
metadata_json: PR #2245 metadata, merged state, changed files, counts
human_pr_text: author PR body naming intended matched functions
raw_diff: complete diff for two changed files
changed_files.jsonl: changed-file metadata
text_corpus: bot report with match percentages and bytes
notes: Evidence is strong for changed files, changed code shape, merge metadata, and bot-reported match outcomes.
There are no human review comments in the loaded slice, so reviewer intent and rationale are not available.
The bot report gives high-level match results but not narrow objdiff/build commands or alternative attempts.
Use of `#pragma dont_inline` and `PAD_STACK(8)` is visible and associated with matched outcomes, but the PR slice does not explain why these tactics were unavoidable.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Shared inline helpers can unlock related function matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: In `ftCo_Damage`, adding `inlineE0` for a repeated air-physics `x221C_b6` dispatch and reworking `inlineD0` to contain its own `x8` guard coincided with new 100% matches for related fly/fly-roll animation and physics functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: inlineD0 and inlineE0 hunks
text_corpus: bot report lists ftCo_DamageFly_Anim, ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim, and ftCo_DamageFly_Phys as new 100.00% matches

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep PAD_STACK and pragma tactics narrow and tied to match evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: This PR used `PAD_STACK(8)` in one function and a scoped `#pragma dont_inline on` around one helper while producing bot-reported matches. The evidence supports recording this as a narrow tactic instance, not as a general style rule.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK and pragma hunks
text_corpus: bot report new matches

source_update_candidates.title: `ftCo_Damage` matching progress in PR #2245
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2245 touched `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Damage.c` and `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`; bot report says the unit `main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Damage` gained 6 new matches including `.sdata2` and several damage fly functions.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl
text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential historical path fact for future matching work; curator should decide whether path_facts tracks PR-specific matching history.

source_update_candidates.title: Document narrow evidence requirement for pragma/PAD_STACK tactic examples
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2245 is an example where `PAD_STACK(8)` and scoped `#pragma dont_inline on` appear in a merged matching PR with bot-reported matches, but the slice lacks author rationale. If standards include examples, this should be framed as an evidence-bound tactic instance, not permission for broad use.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK and pragma hunks
text_corpus: bot report
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing standards already warn that these tactics need evidence; this PR may be useful as a concrete example after curator review.

rejection_notes: Do not infer from this PR that `#pragma dont_inline` is generally acceptable; the loaded evidence only shows one scoped use with a bot match report.
Do not infer that all direct `gobj->user_data` uses should become `GET_FIGHTER`; the diff only supports this in the touched `ftCo_Damage` contexts.
Do not promote the numbered `.sdata2` `@...` labels as a universal naming convention; the evidence only shows specific symbol metadata changes in this PR.
